summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding RavenDB resources to an

members.name: AddDatabase
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<RavenDBDatabaseResource> RavenDBBuilderExtensions.AddDatabase(this IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> builder, string name, string? databaseName = null, bool ensureCreated = false)

summary.text: Adds a database resource to an existing RavenDB server resource.

returns.text: A resource builder for the newly added RavenDB database resource.

builder.text: The resource builder for the RavenDB server.

databaseName.text: The name of the database to create/add. Defaults to the same name as the resource if not provided.

ensureCreated.text: Indicates whether the database should be created on startup if it does not already exist.

name.text: The name of the database resource.

description.text: Thrown when the connection string cannot be retrieved during configuration.
Thrown when the connection string is unavailable.

members.name: AddRavenDB
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> RavenDBBuilderExtensions.AddRavenDB(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name)

summary.text: Adds a RavenDB server resource to the application model. A container is used for local development. This overload simplifies the configuration by creating an unsecured RavenDB server resource with default settings.

children.text: When using this method, a valid RavenDB license must be provided as an environment variable before calling the
methods. You can set the license by calling:

returns.text: A resource builder for the newly added RavenDB server resource.

builder.text: to which the resource is added.

name.text: The name of the RavenDB server resource.

members.name: AddRavenDB
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> RavenDBBuilderExtensions.AddRavenDB(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, RavenDBServerSettings serverSettings)

summary.text: Adds a RavenDB server resource to the application model. A container is used for local development. This version of the package defaults to the
tag of the
container image. This overload simplifies configuration by accepting a
object to specify server settings.

remarks.text: This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts.

returns.text: A resource builder for the newly added RavenDB server resource.

name.text: The name of the RavenDB server resource.

serverSettings.text: containing configuration details for the RavenDB server, such as whether the server should use HTTPS, RavenDB license and other relevant settings.

description.text: Thrown when the connection string cannot be retrieved during configuration.
Thrown when the connection string is unavailable.

members.name: AddRavenDB
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> RavenDBBuilderExtensions.AddRavenDB(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, bool secured, Dictionary<string, object> environmentVariables, int? port = null)

summary.text: Adds a RavenDB server resource to the application model. A container is used for local development. This version of the package defaults to the
tag of the
container image.

remarks.text: This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts.

returns.text: A resource builder for the newly added RavenDB server resource.

environmentVariables.text: The environment variables to configure the RavenDB server.

name.text: The name of the RavenDB server resource.

port.text: Optional port for the server. If not provided, defaults to the container's internal port (8080).

secured.text: Indicates whether the server connection should be secured (HTTPS). Defaults to false.

description.text: Thrown when the connection string cannot be retrieved during configuration.
Thrown when the connection string is unavailable.

members.name: WithDataBindMount

summary.text: Adds a bind mount for the data folder to a RavenDB container resource.

builder.text: The resource builder for the RavenDB server.

isReadOnly.text: Indicates whether the bind mount should be read-only. Defaults to false.

source.text: The source directory on the host to mount into the container.

members.name: WithDataVolume
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> RavenDBBuilderExtensions.WithDataVolume(this IResourceBuilder<RavenDBServerResource> builder, string? name = null, bool isReadOnly = false)

summary.text: Adds a named volume for the data folder to a RavenDB container resource.

builder.text: The resource builder for the RavenDB server.

isReadOnly.text: Indicates whether the volume should be read-only. Defaults to false.

name.text: Optional name for the volume. Defaults to a generated name if not provided.

builder.text: The resource builder for the RavenDB server.

isReadOnly.text: Indicates whether the bind mount should be read-only. Defaults to false.

source.text: The source directory on the host to mount into the container.

builder.text: The resource builder for the RavenDB server.

isReadOnly.text: Indicates whether the volume should be read-only. Defaults to false.

summary.text: Represents secured settings for a RavenDB server, including certificate information and a public server URL.
The password for the certificate file, if required.
The path to the certificate file.

members.name: Secured

certificatePassword.text: The password for the certificate file, if required. Optional.

certificatePath.text: The path to the certificate file.

clientCertificate.text: Optional client certificate used by management code (health checks, ensure-database, etc.) when connecting to a secured RavenDB instance.

domainUrl.text: The public domain URL for the server.

summary.text: Configures licensing options for the RavenDB server.

eulaAccepted.text: Indicates whether the End User License Agreement (EULA) has been accepted. Defaults to

license.text: The license string for the RavenDB server.

summary.text: Advanced/debug optional parameter that forces the TCP scheme for the RavenDB server URL. Useful for deployment scenarios with constrained http(s):// schema usage (e.g., only port 80 can use http)
Gets the licensing options configured for the server.
Optional port to expose for the HTTP endpoint (Studio / REST API). If not set, Aspire will automatically assign a free port on the host at runtime.
The internal URL for the RavenDB server. If not specified, the container resource will automatically assign a random URL.
The setup mode for the server. This determines whether the server is secured, uses Let's Encrypt, or is unsecured.
Optional port to expose for the TCP endpoint (data / cluster traffic). If not set, the default 38888 is used.

enumMembers.name: None
enumMembers.description: No specific setup mode is applied.
